missing_evidence: Product approval for default durable local inbox lifecycle.
Release wording that keeps delivery, routing, and trust claims blocked.
notes: Default persistence must be an explicit product decision, not a side effect of opt-in storage.
required_evidence: Product decision that durable local inbox snapshots should become default lifecycle behavior.
Operator-facing release note preserving that snapshots are read models, not delivery records.

missing_evidence: Forward migration policy for future LocalInboxDurableSnapshot versions.
Fixture tests for upgrade and incompatible snapshot cases before default persistence.
notes: Current schema policy restores JSON-decoded v1 snapshots and rejects unknown versions.
Default persistence still needs forward schema evolution evidence before app restart restore is safe.
required_evidence: Durable snapshot schema versioning policy for current-version restore.
Forward migration, incompatible snapshot handling, and preserve-readable snapshot tests.

missing_evidence: Scheduled cleanup worker or lifecycle hook.
Injected-clock tests for expired and retained snapshot cleanup.
notes: Manual prune exists today; default lifecycle persistence needs automatic cleanup evidence.
required_evidence: Bounded cleanup execution policy with injected clock.
Evidence that expired snapshots are pruned without deleting unexpired snapshots.

missing_evidence: Background-safe write integration for app lifecycle transitions.
Tests or device logs proving interrupted writes fail explicitly or restore safely.
notes: A writer may be lifecycle-safe without enabling background scanning, routing, or fetch.
required_evidence: Mobile lifecycle-safe save policy for foreground/background transitions.
Evidence that writes are bounded and do not imply background BLE operation.

missing_evidence: Android or iOS app restart evidence showing restored LocalInbox read model state.
Replay-normalized fixture tying restored rows back to canonical ingress.
notes: Restored beacon refs must remain unresolved pointers unless a real fetch transport resolves them.
required_evidence: On-device restart fixture restoring policy-approved nearby-message read models.
Fixture evidence for full messages, unresolved refs, gossiped refs, and stale refs.

missing_evidence: Release-candidate artifact bundle containing default persistence lifecycle evidence.
Operator review preserving default persistence limitations.
notes: Release evidence must be attached per release candidate before production wording changes.
required_evidence: Release manifest entries for lifecycle decision, migrations, cleanup, writer, and restore evidence.
Operator review that blocked claims remain visible in release notes.

notes: The current validated local mode keeps app sessions memory-only by default.
Opt-in durable snapshots remain available through explicit operator/session controls.
This plan defines the evidence needed to promote persistence later without changing runtime behavior now.
This is a compatibility policy, not a migration implementation.
Forward migrations still need explicit fixtures before persistence can become a default lifecycle behavior.

rules.requirement: Schema version 1 durable snapshots may be restored after known-key normalization.
Unknown durable snapshot schema versions fail closed.
Durable snapshots without schema_version are not restored.
